questions.question: Choose the option that is unrelated (or odd one out amongst) the given set of numbers.
Choose the option that is unrelated (or odd one out amongst) the given set of words.
questions.explanation: Except oil all other are Milk products.

questions.question: Find the missing term. 13, 27, 55, ?, 223
Arrange the following words as per the order in dictionary. 1. Extent 2. Extend 3. Exterior 4. Extensive 5. Extension
questions.explanation: This is the correct form as per the order in dictionary.

questions.question: From the given alternative words, select the word which can be formed using the letters of the given word only once TRANSGRESS
questions.explanation: Only the word GREAT can be formed by using the given word as all the letters are present in the given word. For the rest of the options either one letter or more than one letter are missing in the given word. For ex in GIFT letters I and F are missing in the given word.

questions.question: If South East becomes South West and North East becomes South East, then which of the following direction will become East?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p2844/image013.jpg) The directions are shifting 90 degrees in the clockwise direction; thus, North becomes East.

questions.question: Select the related word/letter/number from the given alternatives. Steam: 100 degrees:: Ice: ?
questions.explanation: Ice is formed at 0 degrees

questions.question: If E=5 and EAT=120, then EGG=?
questions.explanation: The sum of the 1st 2 digits is multiplied by third digit EGG= (5+7) x 7=12x7=84

questions.question: Arrange the following words in a sequence to form a logical order. a) Knee b) Elbow c) Eye d) Heels e) Tongue
questions.explanation: The sequence will be eye-tongue-elbow-knee-heels

questions.question: In a row of 15 persons, A was 3rd from the right end and B was at a gap of 7 from A, if H was 3rd to the right of B, then what will be H's position from the left end of the row. Assume that all the people are facing the North.
questions.explanation: A's position from the right end= 3rd B's position from the right end= 3+7+1= 11th B's position from left= Total number of persons in the row - position from the right +1 = 15-11+1= 5th Position of H from the left= 5+3 = 8th

questions.question: Identify the diagram that best represents the relationship among the given classes. Humans, doctors, men (a) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image035.png) (b) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image036.png) (c) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image037.png) (d) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image038.png)
questions.explanation: All men and doctors are humans. Some men are doctors. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image039.png)

questions.question: If 24 * 6 - 12 = 8 and 16 * 8 - 2 = 16, then in the expression Z * 8 - 1 = 10, what is the value of 'Z'?
questions.explanation: The logic followed here is: Step I: 24/12 = 2 Step II: 2 + 6 = 8 Similarly, Step I: 16/2 = 8 Step II: 8 + 8 = 16 Similarly, Step I: Z/1 = Z Step II: Z + 8 = 10 Z = 2

questions.question: In the following question, select the related letters from the given alternatives. CBSN : YKJD ∷ RKAL : ?
questions.explanation: C - 4=Y B + 9=K S - 9=J N+16=D Similarly, R - 4=N K + 9=T A - 9=R L+16=B

questions.question: In each of the questions below are given some statements followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: All plants are trees. No tree is a forest. All forests are villages. Conclusions: I. Some forests are plants. II. No village is a tree.
options: If either conclusion I or conclusion II follows.
If neither conclusion I nor conclusion II follows.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/clerkimg/2136/image006.jpg) Neither conclusion I nor conclusion II follows

questions.question: Find the missing term in each of the following series. a b _ c b _ a a _ a _ b
questions.explanation: The pattern is: a b c c b c a a c a b b .

questions.question: Two views of the cube are as given below: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/6700/image016.png) Which number will come opposite to number 6 ?
questions.explanation: The given cube can be unfolded as: 5 126 4 3 So the number opposite to 6 is 1.

questions.question: In the following question, select the related numbers from the given alternatives. 79: 441 ::63:?
How is Rishab's sister's mother's sister's daughter's brother's aunt related to him?
questions.explanation: Rishab's sister's mother is his mother. Now His mother's sister is his aunt, and his aunt's daughter's brother is his cousin. His cousin's aunt may be either his mother or his aunt. So option 4 is the answer.

questions.question: Choose the option that is unrelated (or odd one out amongst) the given set of numbers.
questions.explanation: B (C) D (E F) G (H I J) K N (O) P (Q R) S (T U V) W F (G H) I (J K) L (M N O) P V (W) X (Y Z) A (B C D) E In the third set of letters above, between the first two alphabets, there are 2 alphabets. In other cases, there is only one.

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of number is given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of number given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The column and rows of matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9.A letter from these matrices can be represented first by its row and next by its column, e.g; J can be represented by 01,24,etc and A can be represented by 96,67,etc,similarly,you have to identify the set for the word RAID. MATRIX-I 01234 0RJKIL 1JRLKI 2IKRLJ 3KRLJI 4IJRLK MATRIX-II 56789 5ABCED 6DCABE 7BACDE 8CEDBA 9BACED
questions.explanation: By verifying using 1ST option 00 96 03 40 R A I I By 2nd option 22 55 34 78 R A I D.

questions.question: If a mirror is placed on the given line, then which of the answer figure is right image of the given figure? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image043.png) a) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image044.png) b) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image045.png) c) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image046.png) d) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image047.png)
questions.explanation: In option 1, direction of one inclined arrow is shown wrong. In option 2 direction of one inclined arrow as well as that of smaller horizontal arrow is shown wrong. In option 3, direction of both the inclined arrows is wrong. Option 4 is exact mirror image of the question figure and hence the correct answer.

questions.question: If FRAGRANCE is written as SBHSBODFG, how can IMPOSING be written?
questions.explanation: Each letter in the word is moved one step forward and the first letter of the group so obtained is placed at the end, to obtain the code. So the code would be NQPTJOHJ

questions.question: Find the missing number in the matrix: 633 954 ?75
questions.explanation: Analysing 2nd column, 5*(3-1)-3=7 3RD column, 4*(3-1)-3=5 1st column, 9*(6-1)-6=39

questions.question: A square sheet of paper is folded and then cut as shown in the question figures. Find how the paper will look like when it will be unfolded. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image057.png) a) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image058.png) b) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image059.png) c) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image060.png) d) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image061.png)
questions.explanation: When we unfold it for first time, then the half circle drawn at corner will be completed. On second unfolding, there will be two half circles at two opposite corners. For squares, on first unfolding, there will be 2 squares and there will be four squares on second unfolding. Only option 4 has two half circles and 4 squares. Option 1 and 3 are wrong as they don't have semicircle at corner. Option 2 is wrong as it contains only two squares.
